name: And

aliases.name: And
aliases.comment:
Both a first and second part, conveniently represented as a tuple.

Using this type instead of a tuple
visually unifies it with all the other types such as `List`, `Maybe`, or `Result`.

This in my opinion reads a tad better, especially as a type argument:

    List (Maybe ( String, Int ))

becomes:

    List (Maybe (And String Int))

values.name: firstMap
values.comment: Change the first part based on its current value
values.type: (first -> firstMapped) -> And.And first second -> And.And firstMapped second

values.name: secondMap
values.comment: Change the second part based on its current value
values.type: (second -> secondMapped) -> And.And first second -> And.And first secondMapped

name: AndOr

unions.name: AndOr
unions.comment:
Either [both](And#And) or only the [first or second](Or#Or).

When (not) to use this? → [readme](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-and-or/latest/)

values.name: justOnBoth
values.comment:
`Just` if both values are present, `Nothing` if only one is present

    import Or


    AndOr.Both ( 0, 1 ) |> AndOr.justOnBoth --> Just ( 0, 1 )

    AndOr.Only (Or.First 0) |> AndOr.justOnBoth --> Nothing

    AndOr.Only (Or.Second 1) |> AndOr.justOnBoth --> Nothing

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases
values.type: AndOr.AndOr first second -> Maybe.Maybe (And.And first second)

values.name: justOnOnly
values.comment:
`Just` if only one value is present, `Nothing` if both are present

    import Or


    AndOr.Both ( 0, 1 ) |> AndOr.justOnOnly --> Nothing

    AndOr.Only (Or.First 0) |> AndOr.justOnOnly --> Just (Or.First 0)

    AndOr.Only (Or.Second 1) |> AndOr.justOnOnly --> Just (Or.Second 1)

    AndOr.Only (Or.Second 1)
        |> AndOr.justOnOnly
        |> Maybe.andThen Or.justOnSecond
    --> Just 1

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases
values.type: AndOr.AndOr first second -> Maybe.Maybe (Or.Or first second)

values.name: onBothAlter
values.comment:
In case we have `Both`, change them based on their current values

    import Or


    AndOr.Only (Or.First "hello")
        |> AndOr.onBothAlter (Tuple.mapFirst String.toUpper)
    --> AndOr.Only (Or.First "hello")

    AndOr.Both ( "hello", "there" )
        |> AndOr.onBothAlter (Tuple.mapFirst String.toUpper)
    --> AndOr.Both ( "HELLO", "there" )

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases
values.type: (And.And first second -> And.And first second) -> AndOr.AndOr first second -> AndOr.AndOr first second

values.name: onOnlyAlter
values.comment:
In case we have `Only` one of them, change it based on its current value

    import Or


    AndOr.Only (Or.First "hello")
        |> AndOr.onOnlyAlter (Or.onFirstMap String.toUpper)
    --> AndOr.Only (Or.First "HELLO")

    AndOr.Both ( "hello", "there" )
        |> AndOr.onOnlyAlter (Or.onFirstMap String.toUpper)
    --> AndOr.Both ( "hello", "there" )

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases
values.type: (Or.Or first second -> Or.Or first second) -> AndOr.AndOr first second -> AndOr.AndOr first second

name: Or

unions.name: Or
unions.comment:
One or the other.

When (not) to use this? → [readme](https://dark.elm.dmy.fr/packages/lue-bird/elm-and-or/latest/)

values.name: justOnFirst
values.comment:
`Just` if the first value is present, `Nothing` if the second is present

    Or.First 0 |> Or.justOnFirst --> Just 0

    Or.Second 1 |> Or.justOnFirst --> Nothing

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases

values.name: justOnSecond
values.comment:
`Just` if the second value is present, `Nothing` if the first is present

    Or.First 0 |> Or.justOnSecond --> Nothing

    Or.Second 1 |> Or.justOnSecond --> Just 1

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases

values.name: onFirstMap
values.comment:
In case it's the `First`, change it based on it's current value

    Or.First "hello" |> Or.onFirstMap String.toUpper
    --> Or.First "HELLO"

    Or.Second "hello" |> Or.onFirstMap String.toUpper
    --> Or.Second "hello"

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases
values.type: (first -> firstMapped) -> Or.Or first second -> Or.Or firstMapped second

values.name: onSecondMap
values.comment:
In case it's the `Second`, change it based on it's current value

    Or.First "hello" |> Or.onSecondMap String.toUpper
    --> Or.First "hello"

    Or.Second "hello" |> Or.onSecondMap String.toUpper
    --> Or.Second "HELLO"

Prefer a `case..of` if you handle both cases
values.type: (second -> secondMapped) -> Or.Or first second -> Or.Or first secondMapped

values.name: value
values.comment:
Treat the values from the `First` and `Second` case equally

    [ Or.First 0, Or.Second 1 ] |> List.map Or.value
    --> [ 0, 1 ]
